rulings.Case Title: IN RE: PAUL JAMES GALLIGAN CROMWELL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Writ Of Mandamus Immediate Relief Requested For Caap Application Processing Delays
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar for February 14, 2025, line 1. PETITIONER PAUL JAMES CROMWELL'S Motion For Writ Of Mandamus Immediate Relief Requested For Caap Application Processing Delays. Off calendar for failure to provide courtesy copies per SF Local Rule 2.7B and for failure to provide proof of service pursuant to Rule of Court 3.1300(a), (c). For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: JENNIFER SARKANY ET AL VS. CHRISTIE BARRETT WEST ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Code Compliant Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One, From Defendant Timothy West; Request For Monetary Sanctions As To Defendant Timothy West
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar for February 14, 2025, line 8. PLAINTIFFS JENNIFER SARKANY, RAMSEY ABOUREMELEH, SANDRA FIERRO, NINA ROBIN'S Motion To Compel Code Compliant Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One, From Defendant Timothy West; Request For Monetary Sanctions As To Defendant Timothy West. Plaintiffs' unopposed motion for an order compelling defendant Timothy West to provide further responses to Form Interrogatories, Set One, and for sanctions, is granted. The requests at issue are reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. (CCP Section 2017.010.) Defendant's responses to Form Interrogatory 12.1 and Form Interrogatory 15.1 are incomplete or evasive. Defendant is ordered to provide further verified responses within 20 days of notice of entry of this order. The court finds that Defendant did not act with substantial justification and is ordered to pay sanctions of $500 to Plaintiffs within 20 days of notice of entry of this order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: JENNIFER SARKANY ET AL VS. CHRISTIE BARRETT WEST ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Code Compliant Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set One, From Defendant Timothy West; Request For Monetary Sanctions As To Defendant Timothy West
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar for February 14, 2025, line 8. PLAINTIFFS JENNIFER SARKANY, RAMSEY ABOUREMELEH, SANDRA FIERRO, NINA ROBIN'S Motion To Compel Code Compliant Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set One, From Defendant Timothy West; Request For Monetary Sanctions As To Defendant Timothy West. Plaintiffs' unopposed motion to compel further responses to request for production of documents, set one, from defendant Timothy West, and for sanctions, is granted. The requests at issue are reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. (CCP 2017.010.) Plaintiffs have satisfied their burden of setting forth specific facts showing good cause for production. (CCP 2031.310(b)(1).) These requests relate to the transfer of property at issue in this case. Defendant Timothy West's responses did not contain an affirmation of a reasonably diligent search for information and a specification of whether a particular category of document has been lost or destroyed, as required by CCP 2031.220. Defendant Timothy West is ordered to provide supplemental responses within 20 days of notice of entry of this order. Plaintiffs request sanctions. The court finds that defendant Christie West did not act with substantial justification and awards sanctions of $4175. Defendant shall pay sanctions to Plaintiffs within 20 days of notice of entry of this order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: JENNIFER SARKANY ET AL VS. CHRISTIE BARRETT WEST ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Code Compliant Discovery Responses From Defendant Christie West To Special Interrogatories, Set One And Form Interrogatories, Set One; Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Christie Barrett West
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar for February 14, 2025, line 8. PLAINTIFFS JENNIFER SARKANY, RAMSEY ABOUREMELEH, SANDRA FIERRO, NINA ROBIN'S Motion To Compel Code Compliant Discovery Responses From Defendant Christie West To Special Interrogatories, Set One And Form Interrogatories, Set One; Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Christie Barrett West. The unopposed motion of plaintiffs Jennifer Sarkany et al. (Plaintiffs) for an order compelling defendant Christie West to provide further responses to Special Interrogatories, Set One, and Form Interrogatories, Set One, and for sanctions, is granted in part and denied in part. The requests at issue are reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. (CCP Section 2017.010.) As to Form Interrogatory 15.1, Plaintiff has shown that defendant's response is incomplete or evasive. Defendant Christie West is ordered to provide further verified responses within 20 days of notice of entry of this order. With respect to the Special Interrogatories, however, defendant Christie West has provided verified answers giving straightforward and nonevasive responses that she does not have the information Plaintiffs request. Plaintiffs believe that she should have the information, or doubt the veracity of her statement denying information, but the verified interrogatory responses are sufficient and a motion to compel does not provide a vehicle for the court to test the truthfulness or plausibility of the responses. The motion to compel further responses is denied as to these interrogatories. In light of the mixed results Plaintiffs achieved with this motion, the court finds that circumstances would make imposition of sanctions unjust and does not award sanctions on this motion. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: DANIEL B. POWELL VS. SAN FRANCISCO FIRE DEPARTMENT ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar for February 14, 2025, line 5. DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, LONDON BREED'S DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT. Off calendar. The Zelidon-Zepeda declaration fails to show parties met and conferred by telephone, in person, or by video conference, at least 5 days before the responsive pleading was due, as required by CCP 430.41(a). Both parties are ordered to comply with the Code. The response to the complaint is now due by March 21, 2025. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: ZARINA OSTAPOVA ET AL VS. MURATA ELECTRONICS NORTH AMERICA, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar for February 14, 2025, line 7. DEFENDANT MURATA ELECTRONICS NORTH AMERICA, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Off calendar. The Galek declaration shows that while moving party's counsel attempted to meet and confer with the pleading party, Plaintiff's counsel did not comply with the statutory requirement to meet and confer. Plaintiff's counsel is ordered to comply with the Code and meet and confer in person, by telephone, or by video conference, as required by CCP 435.5(a)(1). (See id. ["As part of the meet and confer process, . . . [t]he party who filed the pleading shall provide legal support for its position that the pleading is legally sufficient, or, in the alternative, how the pleading could be amended to cure any legal insufficiency."].) The response to the complaint is now due by March 21, 2025. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)
